name: NIST Phase 52 Seed Packet
description: 10-page artifact-backed NIST annotation/amendment seed selected by the agentic sweep. Rendered images are ground truth; extraction boxes are candidates to confirm or amend.

pages.label: NIST p.15 · Table of Contents (TOC dotted leaders)
pages.notes: Agent should verify the single Table of Contents parent region; deterministic child toc_entries are nested and spine-verified. Human only amends parent bbox or wrong child row text/geometry.

pages.label: NIST p.27 · tables, footnotes_references, controls_requirements
pages.notes: Inspect visible grid/row/column structure; mark table regions, fragments, captions, and false-positive table-like layouts.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.; Confirm control IDs, enhancements, requirement statements, and whether table cells or paragraphs own the semantic role.

pages.label: NIST p.30 · sections, lists, footnotes_references
pages.notes: Confirm visible section/chapter/appendix headings and whether they should be section anchors or ordinary text.; Mark nested/continued list items and distinguish bullets from prose line wraps.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.

pages.label: NIST p.36 · sections, tables, figures_images_captions
pages.notes: Confirm visible section/chapter/appendix headings and whether they should be section anchors or ordinary text.; Inspect visible grid/row/column structure; mark table regions, fragments, captions, and false-positive table-like layouts.; Confirm actual figures/images/captions; image-object count is only a candidate signal and may include page chrome.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.; Confirm control IDs, enhancements, requirement statements, and whether table cells or paragraphs own the semantic role.

pages.label: NIST p.104 · sections, tables, footnotes_references
pages.notes: Confirm visible section/chapter/appendix headings and whether they should be section anchors or ordinary text.; Inspect visible grid/row/column structure; mark table regions, fragments, captions, and false-positive table-like layouts.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.; Confirm control IDs, enhancements, requirement statements, and whether table cells or paragraphs own the semantic role.; Mark definitions/acronyms/glossary entries and distinguish term labels from descriptive paragraphs.

pages.label: NIST p.421 · sections, footnotes_references, headers_footers
pages.notes: Confirm visible section/chapter/appendix headings and whether they should be section anchors or ordinary text.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.; Identify page chrome separately from body content: headers, footers, page numbers, side labels, and boilerplate.; Mark definitions/acronyms/glossary entries and distinguish term labels from descriptive paragraphs.

pages.label: NIST p.462 · sections, tables, footnotes_references
pages.stratum: sections, tables, footnotes_references, controls_requirements, definitions_glossary, appendix_tables, equations_or_expected_absent
pages.notes: Confirm visible section/chapter/appendix headings and whether they should be section anchors or ordinary text.; Inspect visible grid/row/column structure; mark table regions, fragments, captions, and false-positive table-like layouts.; Confirm whether small bottom text, citations, or standards references are footnotes, references, or body content.; Confirm control IDs, enhancements, requirement statements, and whether table cells or paragraphs own the semantic role.; Mark definitions/acronyms/glossary entries and distinguish term labels from descriptive paragraphs.; For appendix/control summary pages, mark row/column boundaries and merged/continued cells explicitly.; Confirm whether math-like symbols are true displayed equations or ordinary table notation/control text.
